name: Kurant Cheesecake- Absolut
recipeInstructions:
MAKE THE CRUST:
1. Position a rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 325°F (165°C).
2. Cut out two 15 x 12-inch pieces of heavy-duty foil and wrap them around the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
3. In a medium bowl, combine graham cracker crumbs, almonds, sugar, and melted butter. Pat the mixture evenly onto the bottom and sides of the prepared pan. Refrigerate the crust while preparing the filling.
MAKE THE FILLING:
4. In the 4-1/2 qt bowl of a heavy-duty electric mixer using the paddle attachment, beat cream cheese at low speed for 30 to 45 seconds until creamy.
5. Increase the speed to medium and gradually add sugar, one tablespoon at a time, beating until blended about 1 minute more.
6. Beat in eggs, one at a time, scraping down the bowl as necessary.
7. At low speed, beat in cream, Absolut Kurant vodka, and vanilla extract; mix until thoroughly blended about 1 minute.
8. Scrape down the side of the bowl and beat until smooth. Pour batter into chilled crust.
9. Place cheesecake in a roasting pan and place the pan in the oven. Pour hot water into the roasting pan so that the water comes halfway up the side of the springform pan. Bake for 65 to 75 minutes or until firm. Remove springform pan from the roasting pan and place it on a wire rack to cool for one hour.
10. Refrigerate cheesecake for at least 3 hours before preparing topping.
MAKE THE TOPPING:
11. In a microwaveable glass measuring cup, place strawberry jelly. Heat in microwave on high for 1 minute, stirring once until the jelly has melted. Stir in Absolut Kurant vodka.
12. Remove cheesecake from refrigerator and remove side of springform pan. Arrange strawberries over top of cake, covering completely. Using a pastry brush, generously brush strawberries with strawberry/Kurant glaze. Sprinkle top of cake with toasted almonds if desired.
